Title: Ethylene response factor MdERF4 and histone deacetylase MdHDA19 suppress apple fruit ripening through histone deacetylation of ripening-related genes

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Gala; Ralls Janet; Golden Delicious; Orin calli
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts S-adenosyl-L-methionine to ACC, the immediate precursor of ethylene
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Controls climacteric fruit ripening and associated softening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Upregulated in early-maturing cultivar Gala at T4; specifically higher in Gala than in Ralls Janet during ripening
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit flesh during ripening; induced in early-maturing cultivar Gala
Pathway_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: H3K9 acetylation at MdACS3a is higher in early-maturing Gala; MdHDA19 and MdERF4-MdTPL4 promote deacetylation/reduced H3K9ac to repress expression
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point in ethylene biosynthesis; important for timing of ethylene peak
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression promotes ethylene production and fruit softening; silencing decreases ethylene production and increases firmness
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdACS3a was identified as a direct ripening-related target associated with cultivar-specific H3K9ac peaks and transcript upregulation in Gala. 2. MdHDA19 binds the MdACS3a gene region and represses its expression through histone deacetylation. 3. Transient overexpression of MdACS3a promoted ethylene production and fruit softening, whereas silencing reduced ethylene and maintained firmness.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RNA-seq; H3K9ac ChIP-seq; ChIP-qPCR; transient overexpression; VIGS silencing; RT-qPCR; metabolite/ethylene profiling; firmness measurement; GUS reporter assay
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq (24 fruit samples, Illumina NovaSeq6000); H3K9ac ChIP-seq (Illumina HiSeq X-ten); accession not provided
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for tuning harvest maturity and shelf life via editing promoter/chromatin regulation or manipulating ACS activity
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher MdACS3a expression accelerates ripening and softening, likely shortening storage life
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Both Gala and Ralls Janet carried MdACS3a/MdACS3a-G289V alleles; differential ripening here was attributed mainly to regulatory control of MdACS3a expression rather than coding allele background

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: AP2/ERF family transcription factor containing EAR repression motif
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Constitutively active during apple fruit development; allelic variation between early- and late-maturing cultivars
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit development/ripening; tested in apple fruit and calli
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Directly represses MdACS3a expression together with MdTPL4 and MdHDA19 by reducing H3K9 acetylation; EAR motif mutation weakens repression
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional repression and recruitment of histone deacetylation machinery
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Acts by recruiting MdHDA19 to deacetylate H3K9 at target locus MdACS3a; C-to-G mutation in EAR motif reduces deacetylation-associated repression
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes climacteric fruit ripening and softening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: MdERF4 represses ethylene production and delays ripening; MdERF4C/C is associated with late maturity whereas MdERF4C/G is associated with early maturity due to weaker repression
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major allele-dependent repressor that links natural variation in EAR motif to ethylene peak timing and fruit maturity
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The MdERF4 C/G mutant allele is associated with early maturity and earlier ethylene peak. 2. MdERF4 physically interacts with MdHDA19 and works with MdTPL4 to repress MdACS3a. 3. Suppression of MdERF4 increases H3K9 acetylation at MdACS3a, and the EAR motif mutation reduces repression ability of the MdERF4-MdTPL4-MdHDA19 complex.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genotype-phenotype association; BiFC; colocalization; pull-down; transgenic calli RNAi/overexpression; ChIP-qPCR; GUS reporter assay; RT-qPCR; ethylene measurements
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage/ripening timepoint; cultivar genetic background
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; H3K9ac ChIP-seq; accession not provided
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Natural alleles or edited EAR motif variants could be deployed to fine-tune ripening time and storage characteristics
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Weak repression allele accelerates ripening and may reduce shelf life
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A C-to-G mutation in the EAR motif reduces ability to recruit repression machinery; MdERF4-like copy on chromosome 11 (MD11G1252800) was not expressed in fruit

Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed during early apple fruit development
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Fruit flesh during development; tested by transient transformation and in calli
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Represses MdACS3a expression by facilitating H3K9 deacetylation at the MdACS3a locus
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Epigenetic control of ethylene biosynthesis during fruit ripening
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Catalyzes/mediates reduced H3K9ac at MdACS3a; Gala-specific hyper-H3K9ac and reduced repression are linked to early ripening
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes climacteric fruit ripening and fruit softening
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression suppresses ethylene production and delays softening; silencing promotes ethylene production, MdACS3a expression, and ripening
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key epigenetic repressor controlling ethylene biosynthesis through H3K9 deacetylation of MdACS3a
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdHDA19 physically interacts with MdERF4 and localizes with it in the nucleus. 2. Transient suppression of MdHDA19 promotes ripening, ethylene production, and softening, while overexpression has the opposite effect. 3. MdHDA19 directly binds the MdACS3a gene body and represses its expression by decreasing H3K9 acetylation.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Phylogenetic identification; BiFC; subcellular colocalization; pull-down; transient overexpression/silencing in fruit; transgenic calli; ChIP-qPCR with MdHDA19 antibody; H3K9ac ChIP-seq; RT-qPCR; ethylene/firmness assays
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; H3K9ac ChIP-seq; accession not provided
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate for epigenetic engineering or allele selection to delay ripening and extend storage life
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Increasing MdHDA19 may delay desirable ripening and aroma development; reducing MdHDA19 accelerates softening
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A second homolog, MdHDA19-chr05 (MD05G1146000), did not interact with MdERF4 and was not the functional focal copy in this study

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Gala; Ralls Janet; Orin calli; heterologous reporter in Nicotiana benthamiana
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Previously reported constitutively active during apple fruit development
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: MdACS3a via complex with MdERF4 and MdHDA19
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Participates in repression of MdACS3a when assembled with MdERF4 and MdHDA19
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: Recruited by EAR motif of MdERF4
Regulation_Genes.Epigenetic_Regulation: Functions as adaptor enabling chromatin repression complex with MdHDA19
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports repression of MdACS3a and thereby suppresses ethylene production/ripening as part of the complex
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Necessary co-repressor module member linking EAR-motif TF MdERF4 to histone deacetylation machinery
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MdTPL4 is part of the MdERF4-MdTPL4 complex previously shown to function during fruit development. 2. In this study, MdTPL4 was included with MdERF4 and MdHDA19 in reporter assays that repressed MdACS3a-associated GUS activity. 3. The EAR motif mutation in MdERF4 weakens complex-mediated repression, implicating MdTPL4 recruitment as critical.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Prior functional evidence cited; GUS co-transformation assay in Nicotiana benthamiana; interaction context from previous study
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Fruit developmental stage; heterologous transient assay context
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential combinatorial target for ripening control, though direct manipulation may have broad pleiotropic effects
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: As a general co-repressor, perturbation may cause pleiotropic developmental effects
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although central to the repressor complex, most direct functional experiments in this paper focused on MdERF4 and MdHDA19
